content.content:
19-| Mileage Tracking | `mileage_readings`, `meter_events` | Time-series odometer data |
20-| Component Tracking | `train_components`, `component_mileage` | Component-level lifecycle |
21:| PM Management | `pm_types`, `pm_schedule`, `pm_material_requirements` | Maintenance planning |
22-| **Track Capabilities** | `track_capabilities`, `pm_capability_requirements` | **Qualified resource matching** |
23-| **Data Migration** | `pm_baseline_assumptions` | **Cold start and history inference** |
24-| Material Management | `inventory_positions`, `material_reservations` | Parts availability |
25-| Man-hour Planning | `manhour_capacity`, `manhour_projections`, `manhour_adjustments` | Workforce balancing |
26:| Baseline & Drift | `plan_baselines`, `baseline_pm_schedule`, `baseline_mileage_projection` | Plan stability |
27-| Scenario Management | `scenarios`, `scenario_deltas` | What-if analysis |
28-| Costing | `labor_rates`, `material_costs`, `cost_baselines` | Financial tracking |
29:| Audit & History | `pm_schedule_history`, `audit_log` | Change tracking |
30-| Users & Auth | `users`, `roles`, `sessions` | Access control |
31-
32-> **v2.1 Changes:** Added operational constraints domain (service demand, route utilization, train-route compatibility), unavailability events, track capabilities with PM requirements, and data migration assumptions tables.

36-## 3.2 Core Fleet Tables
37-
38-```sql
39--- Enable required extensions
40-CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS "uuid-ossp";
41-CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS timescaledb;
42-
43--- Enumerated types
44-CREATE TYPE fitness_status AS ENUM ('FIT', 'RESTRICTED', 'GROUNDED');
--
446-CREATE TABLE pm_capability_requirements (
447-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
448:    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
449-    capability track_capability NOT NULL,
450-    is_mandatory BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,  -- FALSE = preferred but not required
451-    
452-    notes TEXT,                         -- Explanation of why this capability is needed
453-    
454-    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
455-    
456:    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, capability)
457-);
458-
459:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_cap_req_pm ON pm_capability_requirements(pm_type_id);

462--- Example data:
463--- Track T1 at Bishan has pit, crane, and lifting jacks
464--- INSERT INTO track_capabilities (track_id, capability)
465--- VALUES ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'PIT'),
466---        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'CRANE'),
467---        ('track-t1-bishan-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS');
468-
469--- C4 overhaul requires pit and lifting jacks (mandatory), crane (preferred)
470:-- INSERT INTO pm_capability_requirements (pm_type_id, capability, is_mandatory)
471--- VALUES ('pm-c4-uuid', 'PIT', TRUE),
472---        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'LIFTING_JACKS', TRUE),
473---        ('pm-c4-uuid', 'CRANE', FALSE);

478-## 3.2.6 PM Baseline Assumptions (NEW v2.1)
479-
480-Handles cold start scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration. Tracks assumptions with confidence levels and flags records requiring manual review.
481-
482-```sql
483--- PM history baseline assumptions for cold start scenarios
484-CREATE TABLE pm_baseline_assumptions (
485-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
486-    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
487:    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
488-    
489-    -- Assumption details
490-    assumption_type assumption_type NOT NULL,
491-    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING' - assume first PM at commissioning date + interval
492-    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM' - derived from C4/OH completion implies C1/C2/C3 done
493-    -- 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE' - operator entered value
494-    -- 'IMPORTED' - from legacy system with known quality
495-    -- 'NO_HISTORY' - no data available, using conservative estimate
496-    
497-    -- Assumed baseline values
498-    assumed_last_pm_date DATE,
499-    assumed_last_pm_mileage INTEGER,
500-    confidence_level confidence_level NOT NULL,  -- 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED'
501-    
502-    -- Inference source documentation
--
517-    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
518-    
519:    UNIQUE(train_id, pm_type_id)
520-);
521-
522-CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_train ON pm_baseline_assumptions(train_id);
523-CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_review ON pm_baseline_assumptions(requires_review) 
524-    WHERE requires_review = TRUE;
525-CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_confidence ON pm_baseline_assumptions(confidence_level)
526-    WHERE confidence_level IN ('LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
527-
528--- Example inference rules (implemented in application logic):
529--- 1. If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval is 6 months:
530---    assumed_last_pm_date = 2020-01-01, confidence = 'LOW'
531--- 2. If C4 completed 2024-06-15 and C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3:
532---    For C1/C2/C3: assumed_last_pm_date = 2024-06-15, confidence = 'HIGH'
533--- 3. If no data at all:
534---    assumed_last_pm_date = commissioning_date, confidence = 'ESTIMATED', requires_review = TRUE
--
694-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
695-    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
696:    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
697-    
698-    last_pm_date DATE,
699-    last_pm_mileage_km INTEGER,
700-    next_pm_due_mileage_km INTEGER,
701-    next_pm_due_date DATE,
702-    
703-    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
704-    
705:    UNIQUE(component_id, pm_type_id)
706-);
707-```
708-

711-## 3.5 PM Management Tables
712-
713-```sql
714--- PM types (maintenance task definitions)
715:CREATE TABLE pm_types (
716-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
717-    fleet_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES fleets(id),
718-    pm_code VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,      -- 'C1', 'C2', 'C3', 'C4', 'OVERHAUL'
719-    pm_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
720-    version INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
721-    
722-    -- Interval thresholds (whichever comes first)
723-    interval_km INTEGER,               -- Mileage-based trigger
724-    interval_months INTEGER,           -- Time-based trigger (industry standard)
725-    
726-    -- PM Hierarchy: which lower-level PMs does this PM satisfy?
727-    -- When a C4 is completed, it satisfies C1/C2/C3 requirements
728-    -- This resets their mileage counters from the C4 completion point
729-    -- Example: C4 row has satisfies_codes = ['C1', 'C2', 'C3']
730-    satisfies_codes VARCHAR(20)[],
--
745-    effective_from DATE NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_DATE,
746-    effective_to DATE,                              -- NULL = current version
747:    superseded_by UUID REFERENCES pm_types(id),    -- Link to newer version
748-    
749-    -- Canvas display
750-    display_color VARCHAR(7),
751-    display_priority INTEGER DEFAULT 50,
752-    
753-    active BOOLEAN DEFAULT TRUE,
754-    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
755-    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
756-    
757-    UNIQUE(fleet_id, pm_code, version)
758-);
759-
760--- Index for finding current PM type version
761:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_types_current ON pm_types(fleet_id, pm_code, effective_from DESC)
762-    WHERE effective_to IS NULL AND active = TRUE;
763-
764--- Example PM hierarchy data:
765:-- INSERT INTO pm_types (fleet_id, pm_code, pm_name, interval_km, interval_months, satisfies_codes, ...)
766--- KS Fleet:
767---   C1: interval_km=12500, interval_months=6,  satisfies_codes=NULL
768---   C2: interval_km=50000, interval_months=24, satisfies_codes=['C1']
769---   C3: interval_km=100000, interval_months=48, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2']
770---   C4: interval_km=200000, interval_months=96, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3']
771---   OH: interval_km=800000, interval_months=NULL, satisfies_codes=['C1','C2','C3','C4']
772-
773--- PM material requirements (Bill of Materials)
774-CREATE TABLE pm_material_requirements (
775-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
776:    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
777-    part_number VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
778-    part_description TEXT,
779-    
780-    quantity_per_pm INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
781-    criticality material_criticality NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BLOCKING',
782-    -- BLOCKING = cannot start PM without part
783-    -- DEFERRABLE = can start, must have within X days
784-    -- CONSUMABLE = nice-to-have, can substitute
785-    
786-    lead_time_days INTEGER DEFAULT 14,
787-    substitute_parts VARCHAR(50)[],    -- Alternative part numbers
788-    
789-    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
790-    
791:    UNIQUE(pm_type_id, part_number)
792-);
793-
794:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_materials_type ON pm_material_requirements(pm_type_id);
795-
796--- PM schedule (planned and executed maintenance)
797:CREATE TABLE pm_schedule (
798-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
799-    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
800:    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
801-    
802-    -- Scheduling
803-    target_date DATE NOT NULL,
804-    target_mileage_km INTEGER,
805-    status pm_status DEFAULT 'SCHEDULED',
806-    
807:    -- Compliance window (calculated from pm_types tolerances)
808-    constraint_start DATE NOT NULL,    -- Earliest allowed date
809-    constraint_end DATE NOT NULL,      -- Latest allowed date (statutory lock after this)
810-    
811-    -- Resource assignment
812-    assigned_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
813-    assigned_track_id UUID REFERENCES tracks(id),
814-    assigned_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
815-    assigned_shift shift_type,
816-    
817-    -- Execution
818-    actual_start_date DATE,
819-    actual_end_date DATE,
820-    actual_hours DECIMAL(6,2),
821-    completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
822-    completed_mileage_km INTEGER,
--
839-);
840-
841:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_train ON pm_schedule(train_id, target_date);
842:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_date ON pm_schedule(target_date, status);
843:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_depot ON pm_schedule(assigned_depot_id, target_date) 
844-    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS');
845:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_scenario ON pm_schedule(scenario_id) WHERE scenario_id IS NOT NULL;
846:CREATE INDEX idx_pm_schedule_canvas ON pm_schedule(target_date, train_id)
847-    WHERE status IN ('SCHEDULED', 'IN_PROGRESS') AND scenario_id IS NULL;
848-```

19-| Mileage Tracking | `mileage_readings`, `meter_events` | Time-series odometer data |
20-| Component Tracking | `train_components`, `component_mileage` | Component-level lifecycle |
21:| PM Management | `pm_types`, `pm_schedule`, `pm_material_requirements` | Maintenance planning |
22-| **Track Capabilities** | `track_capabilities`, `pm_capability_requirements` | **Qualified resource matching** |
23-| **Data Migration** | `pm_baseline_assumptions` | **Cold start and history inference** |
24-| Material Management | `inventory_positions`, `material_reservations` | Parts availability |
25-| Man-hour Planning | `manhour_capacity`, `manhour_projections`, `manhour_adjustments` | Workforce balancing |
26:| Baseline & Drift | `plan_baselines`, `baseline_pm_schedule`, `baseline_mileage_projection` | Plan stability |
27-| Scenario Management | `scenarios`, `scenario_deltas` | What-if analysis |
28-| Costing | `labor_rates`, `material_costs`, `cost_baselines` | Financial tracking |
29:| Audit & History | `pm_schedule_history`, `audit_log` | Change tracking |
30-| Users & Auth | `users`, `roles`, `sessions` | Access control |

478-## 3.2.6 PM Baseline Assumptions (NEW v2.1)
479-
480-Handles cold start scenarios where PM history is incomplete during data migration. Tracks assumptions with confidence levels and flags records requiring manual review.
481-
482-```sql
483--- PM history baseline assumptions for cold start scenarios
484-CREATE TABLE pm_baseline_assumptions (
485-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
486-    train_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES trains(id),
487:    pm_type_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES pm_types(id),
488-    
489-    -- Assumption details
490-    assumption_type assumption_type NOT NULL,
491-    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_COMMISSIONING' - assume first PM at commissioning date + interval
492-    -- 'INFERRED_FROM_HIGHER_PM' - derived from C4/OH completion implies C1/C2/C3 done
493-    -- 'MANUAL_OVERRIDE' - operator entered value
494-    -- 'IMPORTED' - from legacy system with known quality
495-    -- 'NO_HISTORY' - no data available, using conservative estimate
496-    
497-    -- Assumed baseline values
498-    assumed_last_pm_date DATE,
499-    assumed_last_pm_mileage INTEGER,
500-    confidence_level confidence_level NOT NULL,  -- 'HIGH', 'MEDIUM', 'LOW', 'ESTIMATED'
501-    
502-    -- Inference source documentation
--
517-    updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
518-    
519:    UNIQUE(train_id, pm_type_id)
520-);
521-
522-CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_train ON pm_baseline_assumptions(train_id);
523-CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_review ON pm_baseline_assumptions(requires_review) 
524-    WHERE requires_review = TRUE;
525-CREATE INDEX idx_assumptions_confidence ON pm_baseline_assumptions(confidence_level)
526-    WHERE confidence_level IN ('LOW', 'ESTIMATED');
527-
528--- Example inference rules (implemented in application logic):
529--- 1. If train commissioned 2020-01-01 and C1 interval is 6 months:
530---    assumed_last_pm_date = 2020-01-01, confidence = 'LOW'
531--- 2. If C4 completed 2024-06-15 and C4 satisfies C1/C2/C3:
532---    For C1/C2/C3: assumed_last_pm_date = 2024-06-15, confidence = 'HIGH'
533--- 3. If no data at all:
534---    assumed_last_pm_date = commissioning_date, confidence = 'ESTIMATED', requires_review = TRUE
--
694-    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT uuid_generate_v4(),
695-    component_id UUID NOT NULL REFERENCES train_components(id),
